sessions.title: USB fan controller board
sessions.description: USB fan board from a salvage pile; warms but motor will not spin consistently.
symptoms: no spin unless wire is wiggled

captures.notes: operator added connector and corrosion close-up evidence

measurements.target: USB ground to motor connector ground

summary.summary_text: Found 3 components on this PCB. Components include: 3 ic_chip. These are candidate detections and class labels should be reviewed against the photo. Visible topology estimate: 2 trace regions and 0 candidate connections at 0.11 confidence. Likely board role: controller_or_embedded_compute at 0.37 confidence with 1 functional block candidate(s). OCR/marking lookup resolved evidence on 3 component(s). Machine connection map: 0 connector candidate(s) at 0.00 confidence. Best salvage/build opportunity: Robot Motor Controller (build_from_salvage). Reference AOI not executed. Topology AOI not executed. Golden image AOI not executed. AOI readiness: research_preview. Evidence certainty: unknown at 0.33 with 26 missing evidence item(s). Project potential: excellent. No specific project recommendations available.

coverage.query: Xbox controller stick drift repair Xbox controller stick drift thumbstick unreliable

top_matches.label: Game controllers
why: high customer demand and many cleaning/repair videos
cleaning, connector, button, flex, USB, and battery faults fit current repair playbooks
analog stick drift now has a dedicated diagnostic and validation lane
salvage/arbitrage workflow can rank lots and donor parts
gaps: calibration software workflow per platform
part compatibility catalog for stick modules, membranes, shells, and batteries
Hall-effect upgrade compatibility and dead-zone tuning knowledge

top_matches.label: Modern game consoles
why: very valuable market, common on repair channels
system can help with triage, visual board evidence, connector inspection, corrosion, fans, power path, and resale math
basic cleaning/thermal service can be made reproducible
gaps: microsoldering workflows for HDMI/USB-C ports and torn pads
boardview/schematic integration per console revision
BGA/APU/storage faults are beyond image-only guidance
firmware/account/drive pairing constraints need model-specific knowledge

coverage.recommendation: Usable target with a narrow workflow; add the listed gaps before marketing it broadly.

device_family.label: Game controller input assembly
evidence: symptom/device hint: controller, drift, stick drift
case phrase: stick drift, xbox controller

rules: start every powered test with a current-limited supply when possible
label connector polarity and voltage before reconnecting loads

subsystem_map.subsystem: analog stick potentiometers or Hall sensors

fault_candidates.name: Analog stick, trigger, or button contact fault
likely_causes: contaminated button membrane or PCB contact

diagnostic_tests.name: Input live test and visual contact inspection
equipment_needed: controller test software or console calibration screen
steps: Record stick center position and button behavior in a live input tester.
Open the controller and photograph screw, flex, and connector locations.
Inspect stick modules, button membranes, and PCB contacts for contamination or wear.
Clean contacts and membranes with electronics-safe alcohol and let them dry fully.
Retest before replacing stick modules.
diagnostic_tests.expected_result: Stick centers repeatably and buttons toggle cleanly.
diagnostic_tests.fault_indicated_if: Axis jitters at rest, does not reach full range, or buttons fail after cleaning.

repair_steps: Clean stick sensor openings, button membranes, and PCB contacts with electronics-safe alcohol.
Replace worn analog stick module if drift remains after cleaning and calibration.
Inspect and reseat flex cables before reassembly.
Run platform calibration or controller test after reassembly.

diagnostic_flow.title: Document before touching
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Preserve connector orientation, labels, screws, and cable routing.
actions: photograph all sides, connectors, switch positions, and labels
record symptoms exactly and whether they are intermittent
mark unknown connectors before unplugging them
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: device state and wiring are reproducible

diagnostic_flow.title: Unpowered inspection
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Find obvious mechanical, thermal, liquid, or solder damage without adding risk.
actions: inspect connectors, cables, switches, and battery compartment
look for burned areas, cracked solder joints, corrosion, bulged capacitors, and missing parts
compare to scan/AOI defect candidates when available
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: no immediate stop-condition damage found
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: repair visible damage or quarantine unsafe battery/high-voltage assembly first

diagnostic_flow.title: Power path check
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm that input power reaches the board and regulated rails are not shorted.
actions: measure input connector polarity and voltage
measure rail-to-ground resistance before applying power
power with current limit and check regulator outputs
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: rails are present, stable, and not current-limited

diagnostic_flow.title: Input module and contact validation
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Separate dirty contacts from worn analog modules or torn interconnects.
actions: record live stick/button behavior before opening
inspect stick modules, membranes, and flex cables under magnification
clean contacts and retest before replacing modules
run platform calibration or a controller tester after reassembly
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: all axes center and reach full range; buttons toggle cleanly

diagnostic_flow.title: Confirm candidate: Analog stick, trigger, or button contact fault
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm the suspected fault before replacing parts.
actions: Record stick center position and button behavior in a live input tester.
Open the controller and photograph screw, flex, and connector locations.
Inspect stick modules, button membranes, and PCB contacts for contamination or wear.
Clean contacts and membranes with electronics-safe alcohol and let them dry fully.
Retest before replacing stick modules.
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: Stick centers repeatably and buttons toggle cleanly.
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: Axis jitters at rest, does not reach full range, or buttons fail after cleaning.

repair_playbooks.title: Analog stick, trigger, or button contact fault
repair_steps: Clean stick sensor openings, button membranes, and PCB contacts with electronics-safe alcohol.
Replace worn analog stick module if drift remains after cleaning and calibration.
Inspect and reseat flex cables before reassembly.
Run platform calibration or controller test after reassembly.
confirmation_after_repair: retest on a current-limited supply
verify fault symptom is gone
run a short thermal check under normal load
record the repair and replaced parts

when_to_stop: you cannot identify the input voltage or polarity
the board immediately hits current limit after disconnecting all loads
a replacement part rating cannot be verified
the repair requires live mains probing and you do not have the proper equipment
evidence_to_collect_next: top-side and bottom-side board photos
connector closeups with wire colors and labels
before/after controller tester readings for stick center, axis range, triggers, and buttons
closeups of analog stick modules, button membranes, flex cables, and PCB contacts
platform calibration or dead-zone validation after reassembly
limitations: This guide is a diagnostic workflow, not proof of a fault until measurements confirm it.
Do not probe mains-powered equipment live. Isolate high voltage before board-level inspection.
Image-only PCB inference cannot see hidden layers, bottom-side components, or exact manufacturer revisions.
repair_guide.quick_summary: Game controller input assembly: start with Document before touching; top candidate is Analog stick, trigger, or button contact fault.

top_matches.label: Battery charging gadgets
why: charging contacts, docks, batteries, fuses, and protection boards fit the evidence workflow
board-in-hand measurements can quickly separate charger, contact, battery, and PCB faults
good fit for repair/restoration videos where the owner has the item in hand
gaps: sealed-device opening procedures and gasket/waterproofing validation
chemistry-specific battery replacement and recycling rules
brand/model battery compatibility catalog

coverage.recommendation: Usable target with a narrow workflow; add the listed gaps before marketing it broadly.

device_family.label: Battery charging gadget or pack
evidence: symptom/device hint: battery, not charging, toothbrush
case phrase: electric toothbrush, not charging

rules: start every powered test with a current-limited supply when possible
label connector polarity and voltage before reconnecting loads

fault_candidates.name: Battery, charge contact, or charge-controller fault
likely_causes: failed power adapter or charging dock
aged battery cell with high internal resistance
blown fuse/thermal link or failed BMS protection board

diagnostic_tests.name: Charger-to-battery path check
steps: Inspect and clean charge contacts or USB/DC jack.
Verify charger output voltage with no device connected.
Measure battery pack voltage and compare with nominal rating.
Check continuity through fuse/thermal link/protection path with power disconnected.
If safe, observe charge current with the correct charger connected.
diagnostic_tests.expected_result: Charger output is correct, battery voltage is plausible, and charge current is within expected range.
diagnostic_tests.fault_indicated_if: No charger output, open fuse/protection path, very low battery voltage, or no charge current.

repair_steps: Clean or repair charge contacts/jack before replacing electronics.
Replace failed adapter/dock if output is wrong.
Replace battery pack/cells only with matching chemistry, protection, and rating.
Replace fuse/BMS/charge-controller parts only after confirming the upstream charger and battery are safe.

diagnostic_flow.title: Document before touching
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Preserve connector orientation, labels, screws, and cable routing.
actions: photograph all sides, connectors, switch positions, and labels
record symptoms exactly and whether they are intermittent
mark unknown connectors before unplugging them
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: device state and wiring are reproducible

diagnostic_flow.title: Unpowered inspection
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Find obvious mechanical, thermal, liquid, or solder damage without adding risk.
actions: inspect connectors, cables, switches, and battery compartment
look for burned areas, cracked solder joints, corrosion, bulged capacitors, and missing parts
compare to scan/AOI defect candidates when available
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: no immediate stop-condition damage found
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: repair visible damage or quarantine unsafe battery/high-voltage assembly first

diagnostic_flow.title: Power path check
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm that input power reaches the board and regulated rails are not shorted.
actions: measure input connector polarity and voltage
measure rail-to-ground resistance before applying power
power with current limit and check regulator outputs
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: rails are present, stable, and not current-limited

diagnostic_flow.title: Charger and battery isolation
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Separate adapter/dock/contact faults from battery pack or board-level charge faults.
actions: verify charger output with no load
inspect and clean charge contacts, dock pins, or USB/DC jack
measure battery/pack voltage without shorting the pack
test with a known-good charger or safe adapter-only mode when available
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: charger, contacts, pack voltage, and charge current are each accounted for

diagnostic_flow.title: Confirm candidate: Battery, charge contact, or charge-controller fault
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm the suspected fault before replacing parts.
actions: Inspect and clean charge contacts or USB/DC jack.
Verify charger output voltage with no device connected.
Measure battery pack voltage and compare with nominal rating.
Check continuity through fuse/thermal link/protection path with power disconnected.
If safe, observe charge current with the correct charger connected.
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: Charger output is correct, battery voltage is plausible, and charge current is within expected range.
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: No charger output, open fuse/protection path, very low battery voltage, or no charge current.

repair_playbooks.title: Battery, charge contact, or charge-controller fault
repair_steps: Clean or repair charge contacts/jack before replacing electronics.
Replace failed adapter/dock if output is wrong.
Replace battery pack/cells only with matching chemistry, protection, and rating.
Replace fuse/BMS/charge-controller parts only after confirming the upstream charger and battery are safe.
confirmation_after_repair: retest on a current-limited supply
verify fault symptom is gone
run a short thermal check under normal load
record the repair and replaced parts

when_to_stop: battery is swollen, punctured, hissing, smoking, or unusually hot
you cannot identify the input voltage or polarity
the board immediately hits current limit after disconnecting all loads
a replacement part rating cannot be verified
the repair requires live mains probing and you do not have the proper equipment
evidence_to_collect_next: top-side and bottom-side board photos
connector closeups with wire colors and labels
charger rating and no-load output voltage
battery pack voltage compared with nominal rating
charge current or dock current draw during safe charging test
charge-contact closeups before and after cleaning
input voltage measurement
rail-to-ground resistance before power-up
limitations: This guide is a diagnostic workflow, not proof of a fault until measurements confirm it.
Do not probe mains-powered equipment live. Isolate high voltage before board-level inspection.
Image-only PCB inference cannot see hidden layers, bottom-side components, or exact manufacturer revisions.

evidence_tasks.prompt: motor/load resistance and isolation reading
connector continuity while gently flexing the harness
driver output voltage with current limit and dummy load
connector closeups with wire colors and labels
top-side and bottom-side board photos

coverage.query: USB fan motor no-spin export smoke USB fan controller board warm board motor will not spin works if connector is wiggled

top_matches.label: Game controllers
why: high customer demand and many cleaning/repair videos
cleaning, connector, button, flex, USB, and battery faults fit current repair playbooks
analog stick drift now has a dedicated diagnostic and validation lane
salvage/arbitrage workflow can rank lots and donor parts
gaps: calibration software workflow per platform
part compatibility catalog for stick modules, membranes, shells, and batteries
Hall-effect upgrade compatibility and dead-zone tuning knowledge

top_matches.label: Small USB powered gadgets
why: excellent match for low-voltage repair encyclopedia lane
power, connector, regulator, driver, load, and cable faults are covered
safe current-limited diagnostic flow is straightforward
gaps: device-specific enclosures and mechanical replacement parts
load libraries for motors, fans, pumps, heaters, and LEDs

coverage.recommendation: Usable target with a narrow workflow; add the listed gaps before marketing it broadly.

rules: start every powered test with a current-limited supply when possible
label connector polarity and voltage before reconnecting loads

fault_candidates.name: Driver stage or motor/load path fault
likely_causes: open motor winding or disconnected harness
missing flyback/protection diode causing repeated driver damage

diagnostic_tests.name: Load resistance and isolation check
steps: Disconnect power and unplug the motor/load harness.
Measure resistance across the motor/load leads.
Measure each motor/load lead to board ground.
diagnostic_tests.expected_result: Finite winding/load resistance and no short to ground unless designed that way.
diagnostic_tests.fault_indicated_if: Near-zero resistance, open circuit, or unexpected ground short.

diagnostic_tests.name: Driver output test with current limit
steps: Power the board through a current-limited supply.
Command the output while using a dummy load before reconnecting the real motor.
Measure output voltage across the load terminals.
diagnostic_tests.expected_result: Output switches cleanly without current-limit collapse.
diagnostic_tests.fault_indicated_if: No switching, supply current limit trips, or driver overheats.

repair_steps: Reseat or replace damaged harness/connectors.
Replace failed MOSFET/transistor/relay with equal or higher voltage/current rating.
Replace or add flyback/protection diode for inductive loads.
Retest with dummy load before reconnecting the motor or external load.

fault_candidates.name: Connector, solder joint, or harness intermittency

diagnostic_tests.name: Wiggle and continuity test
steps: Power off and inspect connector solder joints under magnification.
Measure continuity from connector pin to next visible node.
Flex the cable gently while observing continuity.
diagnostic_tests.expected_result: Stable continuity with no visual cracking.
diagnostic_tests.fault_indicated_if: Continuity drops, joint ring cracks are visible, or cable movement changes behavior.

repair_steps: Clean contacts with electronics-safe contact cleaner.
Reflow cracked connector joints with flux.
Replace damaged cable or connector shell.
Add strain relief before final reassembly.

diagnostic_flow.title: Document before touching
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Preserve connector orientation, labels, screws, and cable routing.
actions: photograph all sides, connectors, switch positions, and labels
record symptoms exactly and whether they are intermittent
mark unknown connectors before unplugging them
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: device state and wiring are reproducible

diagnostic_flow.title: Unpowered inspection
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Find obvious mechanical, thermal, liquid, or solder damage without adding risk.
actions: inspect connectors, cables, switches, and battery compartment
look for burned areas, cracked solder joints, corrosion, bulged capacitors, and missing parts
compare to scan/AOI defect candidates when available
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: no immediate stop-condition damage found
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: repair visible damage or quarantine unsafe battery/high-voltage assembly first

diagnostic_flow.title: Power path check
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm that input power reaches the board and regulated rails are not shorted.
actions: measure input connector polarity and voltage
measure rail-to-ground resistance before applying power
power with current limit and check regulator outputs
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: rails are present, stable, and not current-limited

diagnostic_flow.title: Output/load isolation
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Separate a bad board driver from a bad motor, relay, solenoid, cable, or load.
actions: disconnect the load harness
measure load resistance and check for shorts to chassis/ground
test the board output with a dummy load before reconnecting the real load
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: dummy load switches correctly and real load measures sane resistance

diagnostic_flow.title: Confirm candidate: Driver stage or motor/load path fault
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm the suspected fault before replacing parts.
actions: Disconnect power and unplug the motor/load harness.
Measure resistance across the motor/load leads.
Measure each motor/load lead to board ground.
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: Finite winding/load resistance and no short to ground unless designed that way.
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: Near-zero resistance, open circuit, or unexpected ground short.

diagnostic_flow.title: Confirm candidate: Connector, solder joint, or harness intermittency
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm the suspected fault before replacing parts.
actions: Power off and inspect connector solder joints under magnification.
Measure continuity from connector pin to next visible node.
Flex the cable gently while observing continuity.
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: Stable continuity with no visual cracking.
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: Continuity drops, joint ring cracks are visible, or cable movement changes behavior.

repair_playbooks.title: Driver stage or motor/load path fault
repair_steps: Reseat or replace damaged harness/connectors.
Replace failed MOSFET/transistor/relay with equal or higher voltage/current rating.
Replace or add flyback/protection diode for inductive loads.
Retest with dummy load before reconnecting the motor or external load.
confirmation_after_repair: retest on a current-limited supply
verify fault symptom is gone
run a short thermal check under normal load
record the repair and replaced parts

repair_playbooks.title: Connector, solder joint, or harness intermittency
repair_steps: Clean contacts with electronics-safe contact cleaner.
Reflow cracked connector joints with flux.
Replace damaged cable or connector shell.
Add strain relief before final reassembly.
confirmation_after_repair: retest on a current-limited supply
verify fault symptom is gone
run a short thermal check under normal load
record the repair and replaced parts

when_to_stop: you cannot identify the input voltage or polarity
the board immediately hits current limit after disconnecting all loads
a replacement part rating cannot be verified
the repair requires live mains probing and you do not have the proper equipment
evidence_to_collect_next: top-side and bottom-side board photos
connector closeups with wire colors and labels
motor/load resistance and isolation reading
connector continuity while gently flexing the harness
driver output voltage with current limit and dummy load
input voltage measurement
rail-to-ground resistance before power-up
current draw at startup under current limit
limitations: This guide is a diagnostic workflow, not proof of a fault until measurements confirm it.
Do not probe mains-powered equipment live. Isolate high voltage before board-level inspection.
Image-only PCB inference cannot see hidden layers, bottom-side components, or exact manufacturer revisions.
repair_guide.quick_summary: Small DC motor / actuator gadget: start with Document before touching; top candidate is Driver stage or motor/load path fault.

sessions.title: USB fan warms but motor will not spin value trial
sessions.description: operator has the gadget and is using photos as scans, not as the only evidence

captures.notes: operator attached harness, connector, and solder-joint closeups

measurements.notes: supply holds up while fan fails to spin

evidence_tasks.prompt: motor/load resistance and isolation reading

review.notes: voltage USB input under attempted startup = 4.91 V

evidence_tasks.prompt: connector continuity while gently flexing the harness

review.notes: operator confirmed cracked solder at motor harness

evidence_tasks.prompt: driver output voltage with current limit and dummy load
connector closeups with wire colors and labels
top-side and bottom-side board photos

review.notes: operator attached harness, connector, and solder-joint closeups

payload.notes: operator confirmed cracked solder at motor harness

outcomes.notes: reflowed harness joint and fan spins normally

coverage.query: USB fan warms but motor will not spin value trial USB fan controller board motor will not spin warm board wire wiggle changes behavior operator has the gadget and is using photos as scans, not as the only evidence
